reviews.review_text:
the beauty of dome keeper is that there are no prerequisites. you don’t need to be in the zone or firing on all cylinders; it’s always there for a quiet 30-minute run, no questions asked.

a game whose depth is revealed gradually, almost sneakily. you’re stranded on an alien planet, your dome is the only thing keeping you alive, and waves of monsters are on their way. between attacks, you drill down into the earth in search of resources like iron, cobalt, and water, bring them back up, and spend them on upgrades. then the next wave hits. then you do it again.

every second you spend underground is a second you’re not at your dome’s controls. do you push deeper for the better materials, or play it safe and head back early? that risk-reward pull never really goes away, and it keeps every run feeling alive even when you’re technically doing the same things over and over.

the roguelike elements help a lot here. each run is procedurally generated, gadgets and upgrades vary, and there’s a meta-progression system that lets you bring permanent perks into future runs. it doesn’t reinvent the wheel, but it’s well-tuned; you feel like you’re getting better at the game and getting a stronger character at the same time. as you keep playing, you unlock new domes, new worlds, new shields; each one comes with its own fun twist that actually changes how you approach a run. each new addition makes you think differently, experiment with new strategies, and find a new favourite setup all over again.

it’s also worth mentioning how atmospheric the game is for something so small. the crash-landing setup, the lonely planet, the sense that you’re just trying to survive long enough to get out. it works.

the latest updates allows you to defend and mine together with friends, and this is honestly where the game has resonated with me the most personally. there’s a particular kind of joy in coordinating with someone, one of you drilling deep while the other holds down the dome. it breathes new life into an already great game.

I’d usually be in favour of waiting for a sale, but at $17.99, it doesn’t really drive a hard bargain to get it at full price.

this one took me by surprise. a friend recommended dome keeper to me, I was not sold, like, at all. with due time, I went ahead and tried the demo, which foretold a different tale.

I intend to do more with this game!

Great concept. I am generally a fan of titles with multiple gameplay states (in this case, mining + dome defense).
Skill floor is generally very low, but skill ceiling is quite high (for some miners) givng you the chance to sit back and relax, or tryhard if you so wish.
Huge fan of the meta progression. Offers a true roguelike experience (by not having persistent character upgrades) yet still has unlocks to enhance difficulty and add some gameplay variety.
Highly recommended.

Only issues from my end are:
-Replayability. Despite the unlocks available, the core gameplay remains quite similar accross runs
-Controls. Playing infiltrator with the stock controller settings is a nightmare
-Miner compatibility. Beatmaster especially required walls to move, meaning that the more terrain you destroy, the harder it is to move around the map. This is mostly a problem in co-op with Assessor. I do appreciate that the catgoblins do not break terrain, but just extract resources. Show great attention to detail.

Simple game style and play however got some room to master the game for those min max style people, easy to just pull up and play with friends, and some progressive progress to earn when playing the game. Overall a simple game that is at the very least 8/10 for what it is, but could be a as good as a 10/10 for those who like this type of game.

I do recommend it.

The game was average before, but the free multiplayer update made it top notch. Simple arcade mechanics, but well explored. Price point is really affordable at discounts, and you should definitely get it if you have a group of friends to cooperate with you.

If you have tendinitis, carpal tunnel or any sort of hand condition, you may want to be careful. Because the same mechanic from the core loop requires you to keep constantly moving around and pushing against the walls. So your left thumb (gamepad) or ring, middle, index fingers (keyboard) will be in a restless state permanently during the whole game session. This causes long-term damage.

If your monitor is ultra-wide (32:9) and you are playing at fullscreen, you can't operate the cannon when you split the screen specifically for 2 players. That happens because the game zooms out when the player viewport aspects are vertical. Essentially, it renders the game unplayable under that specific condition, either that or you are forced to downscale the video settings to a 16:9 window.

Aside from that, game is extra-lightweight and the splitscreen code is spotless and frictionless. Press a button? Screen is instantly split. Want to disconnect that player? Start >> Disconnect. Pretty straightforward.

It also support one of the rarest features in games: Combo Co-op. You can mix N local players with M online players (N + M = 4). I have only seen this from Arrowhead (Gauntlet, Helldivers 1...), since it is so hard to code that feature without breaking the whole game.

A game I bought with no expectations and one I keep finding myself thinking about when I'm busy with other things. For me, it required no guides to understand the basics and it's fun, in my opinion, to just go out and find out the secrets by just coming across it. Not even knowing what can be unlocked or learned. I don't know how else to say that it all just works, the music, the simplicity, the atmosphere of it.
One of the most unique games I've played in a while. And the devs keep on updating it! Would absolutely recommend!

Good roguelike for players who enjoy mining resources and managing supplies while fending off waves of monsters in between. Core gameplay loop is quite engaging thanks to its originality but it starts to feel somewhat repetitive after a while. Still, the wide variety of gadgets, upgrades, and game modes provides enough content to keep you entertained for dozens of hours.

Simple premise, addictive loop.
Has been a longtime favorite "solo gaming" choice especially for end of day, low energy gaming BUT now it even has a multiplayer mode update so you can play with or against friends/strangers.
Great art, great music, great price.
Much love to the Devs! <3
Fun solo or Co-op, great way to kill time, dig deep and find the ore!!!

Dome Keeper is a fun resource management and roguelike game

There are four different game modes for a good amount of variety and I really enjoyed completing all of the challenges in co-op with my friend

I think everyone should purchase the Lost Keepers DLC because the new characters are way more fun to play than the original ones

The only downside to this game is the balancing. Some weapons, abilities, defense systems are way better than others. So I ended up playing the same build over and over again with a few changes depending on the challenge level selected.

I hope the developer releases more DLC for different defense towers, characters to play, and more challenge levels. I would love to revisit this game in the future :)
its a labor of love and it shows. lota' fun with great polish

I've banged out about 90 hours on this game and I love it. very chill. good sounds, there are exciting moments. every wave feels like triumph in itself. I know that at the time of writing this it is about six dollars after tax. The game is worth your time and the devs deserve more attention.

Simple looking game (Which I love) with complex mechanics. Love how you need to manage your resources between being able to drill deeper and fending off waves of monsters. Co-op mode is fun aswell, and can enhance your experiance whether your are playing with or against someone. Finally a simple little detail I like is the animation for the relic. Would definately recommend to anyone who loves having fun alone or with friends!
Fun little gameplay loop. Good for a few hours of entertainment if you buy it cheap. Feels like a good flash game.
Nice gameplay, cool music. Amazing timesink

Very addicting roguelite, but leaning very close to a roguelike b/c the new unlocks and permanent upgrades from guild assignments are not as powerful as in other roguelites out there.

The gameplay loop is mining resources to buy new ways to help you mine resources faster or to upgrade your dome's defenses to fend off waves of enemies that come after a set amount of time.

Also, the game doesn't throw you into a bunch of new systems or controls all at one from the start, which was very appreciated. 

The game lets you customize your runs' difficulty level. You're free to make the runs as easy as you want to just go for quick gadget unlocks or tackle guild assignments for permanent upgrades.

hidden gem this game is PEAK very fun to defend the dome from monsters with different gadgets and dome types. game gets even funner when unlocking the next character and all the domes and primary gadgets. Very VERY fun game
Fantastic little indie game. So many options and potentials for runs, fantastic soundtrack and it keeps me coming back because it has just enough RNG to make each run feel unique.
Great gameplay loop.  Dig, Fight, Repeat!
A really fun indie game! Can run on low-end laptops.

I dont think that there is any other game that is so chill at moments but changes to hardcore so fast! I got this on discount but i think it's still worth 18 dollars. Also the achivements are very fun. Another thing is this game has a workshop???
cool rougelike game, especially on steamdeck. makes me want to play just one more run

All I can say... is that this game is SO PEAK
I cannot explain how much fun this game is, ESPECIALLY with friends, I HIGHLY suggest you play this with four or five friends for one of the best experiences of your life
10/10
its a really fun game to play on the steamdeck and when i got nothing else important going on

It's so good!
Kind of reminds me of Turrian3000 but tower defence but mining...
It's amazing.  Devs should be proud.
Suprisingly mindless. In a bad way. But the game still is fun somehow. maybe a 7/10

I like a lot of dungeon delving games but rarely do I opt for "defensive space invader" style games. 

Each game can be 10-60 minutes depending on your world size which is completely customizable before the game run begins. I love speculating in the menu what I'm going to embark on each run, will I opt for searching Relics or perhaps a challenge given by the higher ups who sent me to these worlds.

The fundamental gameplay loop relies on balancing your time evenly by exploring the depths below and returning to the surface to defend the waves of enemies inbound, the waves are not necessarily difficult but they can get overwhelming when not timed correctly with your Dome upgrades. It's especially rewarding to wait and let the tiny insect enemies swarm your Dome and unleash a powerful electric shock or creating a sonic blast by smacking hammers together.

So far the game has far exceeded my expectations, each delve I encounter something new and unique, there's been very little repetition other than the basic resource gathering and upgrades. It's vast, full of collectables, well thought out upgrades and small little quality of life bonuses that makes it perfect for the Steam Deck and competes well with other Roguelike games.

I've not played multiplayer but I can assume it would be a wonderful experience with friends.
Easy to play, lots of options for challenges.

This game is a work of art!
I have been playing it on and off through the past years and decided to return now that it has multiplayer/co-op!
I love the TD/mining/Roguelike approach this game offers!
It is as chill or chaotic as you want it to be!
So many things left to discover and explore and master as it is easy to learn yet hard to master!
W Devs btw

I like the game a lot. I will keep playing but using the mortar dome lags the crap out of my m3 macbook with 18gb of ram. Im not sure whats goin on but its a HUGE isssue. also the ninja class feels bad to play

i really recommend this game it's been very fun! however, recent bugs are bothering my sessions a lot:
-  getting kicked out of the game randomly. it happens at least once every 5 minutes and causes my resources to scatter and monster waves to restart
- graphic issues like gadgets lingering on the screen despite already being implemented and the arm of the beast stretching across the screen
Domekeeper is a great and creative game which is great with music in the backround! Should be worth more money because of how good it is, though-

For my spouse and I this is our new favorite co-op game. 
I had already been playing for a bit and seen her watching and interested. Once they added split  screen co op she joined in. She loves to go around and mine while I take care of the waves and collect stuff. 
Tons of fun.
Exceptional mining/wave combat roguelike!  Addicting & satisfying gameplay flow - varied class mechanics.  Excellent indie gem!

Great game that's very flexible: whatever I'm looking for, whether it's a harsh resource management game or a chill mining sim, I can get. While I have my favorite combination of dome and gadgets for sure, it's fun to mix it up and try different setups for the domes for different challenges, and I enjoy the little long-term upgrades you can get from the guild challenges. Multiplayer is currently a little buggy, but I hope that will stabilize soon. Highly recommend!
Casual, satisfying, and addictive. It's simultaneously both shallow and deep at the same time. I refuse to elaborate on that. Definitely worth buying on blu ray.
Neat little game about 2 things, mining down efficiently, and then rushing up to the surface at the last moment to fight off nightly waves of badies. Then teching up and diving back down.

Absolutely fascinating roguelike. When you're comfortable, it's one of the most simple, relaxing, yet engaging games on the market, absolutely perfect for decompressing after a long day. But when you're not, it's a frantic, tense experience that will push your logistical skills, planning, and tactile execution to the limits. My only issues lie with the performance, since the game does seem to make every other program on my computer (especially youtube videos which I like to watch on the side while playing) absolutely explode, and it can start hitching periodically once you get deep into a run. Besides that, this game is phenomenal. Absolute must-have for any fans of that old flash game Motherlode.

It's like relaxing resource digging and base defense combined, and the challenges are also fun. With recent multiplayer update you can have even more fun by defending together or stealing stuff from each other

I'm thinking of the larger playerbase when I do this review. The Rng on map setups has alot possibilities, so much where it can be a detriment to the game. I wish there was a little more warning before I find out the game is a bust? The community that plays these roguelike games tend to love what I'm currently struggling to give a positive review for.
Good:
A good diversity in gadgets and domes with supplementary upgrades during the mission to really give you options on the fly.
The game modes are also fun, different variations of "fetch" missions. With some of the variants being "fetch for the guild" which really has me thinking about different ways I would "harm" a guild member if I had the opportunity... I got broken comms? Lets go into this deep ass mine because your greedy...
The different miners with variant styles are also really good to see. I'm a basic ♥♥♥♥♥ that likes the miner, but the beast master looks fun. WTF is the ninja dude? He moves like I'm trying to crawl out of bed in the morning.
Bad:
Some maps can be a complete waste of time and I don't know I'm wasting my time until i'm like 6 waves in. I could be working a job that hates my guts instead of working a job that hates my guts...
The early reliance on Iron makes the Iron Dense modifier only done with a certain set of Extra gadgets like the Suit Blaster. It takes a T3 Drill to destroy 1 block in a reasonable amount of time at the top of the mine. I tested it and yeah, that's way to hard. 
The maze modifier is a toss up to wasting your time to getting a smooth run. Alot of the time I get waste of time because the resources are spread apart and in distant places, making me rely on speed just to get in and out on time, which leaves me without drill upgrades.
I can go on here, but I don't think I need to. Alot of issues tend to be weak upgrades on the keepers end, and ridiculous difficulty gaps.
Example of Solutions:
Maze maps- Give us a slight speed upgrade to get around.
Dense Iron- Make it Tier system in difficulty. Normal modifier is T2 to break early brick. T3 to do challenge.
I haven't even gotten that far into the game to even speak on the rest, but the difficulty in general needs to be looked at.
Conclusion:
I can appreciate the game, but I don't like wasting time with these toss up maps that you can't get traction on. Again, the community that like procedural generation will die for a game like this because that's half the fun for them. I'm to autistic for this ♥♥♥♥ lol.
Pick up this game to play with the family, but after play a few times truly love play all by myself.
Another game worth sinking time into. Fun and a great way to kill time.

Very task-oriented game. Simple idea, but enough variation to keep things interesting. Highly customizable settings to the tastes of the players (you can even turn off enemies, and then I guess it just becomes a cozy mining game).
its a very fun game with or without friends, so just get the game and it didn't lag at at all

[h1]Didn't expect to get this addicted[/h1]

I don't usually play games like this, but Dome Keeper hooked me almost instantly.

The gameplay loop is insanely fun, the coop with friends is great, and you can really tell this game was made with care and attention to detail.

[hr][/hr]

[b]Amazing value for the price.[/b]

Definitely one of those indie games where you start playing "for a bit" and suddenly 3 hours are gone.

As cozy as it gets. Running the same upgrades start of each loop gets a little stale but the different monsters, keepers, domes, modules, and challenges far make up for it. Everything is well designed and clearly play-tested which is a treat nowadays. Love the style and sound design.
This was already a pretty good game. Then they had to go and add multiplayer. Legendary devs.

Highly recommended. Very addicting game that can be enjoyed for short periods or you can get lost playing it for hours. The gameplay loop of mining for resources and then rushing back to your dome to fight off waves of monsters. Even though the game keeps you on your toes it’s extremely cozy

Highly recommend this game. It's the type of game that can be enjoyed for short, ~15 minute sessions, or you can get lost playing it for hours. I really like the gameplay loop of mining for resources and then rushing back to your dome to fight off waves of monsters. Even though the game is requires speed and violence, it's surprisingly chill and cozy.
Probably the best in its genre. Wide range of content from totally chill to edge of panic. I really hope it gets more DLC.

Awesome game, the multiplayer still has a few bugs but it'll be amazing in a few weeks. I loved playing on my own ( 70 hours or so) , but now that I can play with my friends and family it's gotten so much better!
great game. nothing more to say.

This game is a lot of fun - especially now that they've added splitscreen coop. It has a satisfying gameplay loop with some novel mechanics that I haven't seen before and enough variety in game modes that it won't get boring anytime soon. Well worth checking out, especially if you've got someone to play with.
It is a very pretty game that has me doing the same stuff for the most part over and over but still makes it enjoyable.
Good game, gets repetitive after you unlock everything though.
very interesting game to play, trains your mind to think better and critically
amazing game. Love the gameplay, the mining as well as the tower defense gameplay!
Good game. Runs well on PC with an older GPU and Steam Deck. Don't have the DLC yet, but it's probably fun.
I go back to this game often. Especially as a game to play on my Steam Deck. It's a lot of fun

Dear pretty please developers, add:
”you asked for it +1” 
”You asked for it +2” 
Etc for Xbox relic hunt 
Me and my friend have been playing this game for ages and it’s simply too easy for us in the past year. Prestige is not the same

Pretty please ? :3
been following this game for ages and it just keeps getting better w every update love u guys keep fighting the good fight amen
Great game, simple but fun and repeatable.

Dome Keeper blends calm relaxing mining and 2d shooter aspects into one game. The fact that the game added multiplayer just makes this game even better having roles and helping each other makes this game great.
Good game. I ended completing half the game without any perks. It was a lot of fun, but it started to get fairly easy after I applied them.

This game is awesome! I love its replayability, it can be really relaxing to play if you just need to wind down because of the somewhat repetitive gameplay loop (which isn't as boring as you'd expect, because of all the different artifacts and abilities you can unlock throughout the level to try different strategies), if you prefer more a challenge, there's different quests and difficulties you can play. I also love the different characters you can play with their own set of unique playstyles, it really makes the game all that more interesting! Overall, I love the way the game is going so far! I haven't been able to try out the multiplayer modes yet, but even in singleplayer this game is great!
Good game, but it gets pretty redundant pretty fast.

This is one of those games that you start playing and before you know it you're 2 hours deep. Great mix of repetitive, consistent objectives but with a seemingly innumerable amount of modifiable caveats to implement. Highly recommend this title!

This game has a tight gameplay loop and runs well, and it's clear the developers know what they're going for. I'm personally not having much fun with it, but I can't in good conscience give it a thumbs down.
Good fun. The game is very well made, my friend and I havent encountered any bugs and all the mechanics are very impressively implemented.
The game is very fun and splitscreen implementation is really good. Perfect for some couch coop with a friend.

Played the old Motherload game back in the day when I was a kid and I thought this was quite identical. 

Let my 5 yr old daughter play and surprisingly she likes it even though it was singleplayer.

Now that it can be played 4 player coop, Me and my daughter are playing it, I love how she finds the drones and pets cute . Good game to play with friends and family
I like keeping da dome with my friend.....I'm the engineer.....tap tap tap.....oh golly my fruit's about to run out! zip zip zip.......

What a fun game! 

I'm really not usually a fan of roguelikes/lites but this game is such a good implementation of those mechanics, I didn't mind it at all. 
The keepers are all well designed and feel different from each other to play, each has their own strengths and all are satisfying to upgrade and play. 

The Dome types/Gadgets are all fun to use and I've really enjoyed figuring out which work well together and which will be best for each guild assignment. 

Honestly coop was such a good addition and I've had a lovely time playing with my partner as a chill game for the evenings.
this game extremely fun and entertaining. it will keep you occupied for a long tie and it has multiplayer!

i recommend. game has massively improved over the years, when i first started it was only laser and sword, shield and repellant; just engineer and assessor. relic hunt only. i have loved to see dome keeper get better after coming back a couple of years later. its extremely replayable, the challenges and new characters are extremely fun. p much my only complaint is how dominant tesla feels over everything else. and we dont even talk about artillery.

Got this game after the multiplayer update and me and my friends are still having a lot of fun completing the guild quests. The gameplay loop is really solid.

There are a good number of domes and gadgets to unlock, but you can unlock most of them in the first few hours. After that, the game starts to feel a bit repetitive. Is that bad? No, not really. For the price, the game offers a great deal of content.

Lastly, I’d love to see more keepers & domes added in the future, hopefully as part of the base game.

Pirated on EA, played a lot of runs, boughted now, recommend

wasd game about digging :D

Pros: DIGGING, pixel art style, simple mechanics, meta progression
Cons: slow meta prog (i feel like it)

The loop here is tight, the modes offered are plentiful and the there is enough variation in here to have fun with the long time. The multiplayer update adds a whole range of replyability, taking on challenges and working as team in a way to synchronise the miner types adds a whole new layer of depth. Not to mention the dome types keep the fights interesting enough it hasnt felt repetitive even after >30 hours.
Many different game modes, map sizes, characters, items, upgrades to uncover and play with. Very much recommended.
One of the best games I've ever played with a killer art style to boot. Can't recommend it enough! Love to see the updates make the game even better over the years.
I really was skeptical starting to play this but it is super fun. It isn't action packed but I really like it. So if you're on the fence then maybe you'd actually really like it too.

Tower defense but in between waves, you go dig for resources to buy your upgrade. very good gameplay loop. Mining feels nice after a few upgrades but the tower defense aspect is very fun. I dont know if there is an endless dome defend mode but it makes me want that as a mode. Big thumbs up.

Beautiful game. Wonderful art. The ambient noise and music are top notch. The mechanics are very well thought out. Easy to keep playing, and it really makes you WANT to unlock everything. A great game for casual folks as well as completionists.
it is super fun and addictive and great to come back to every couple of months
saw a trailer, reminded me of motherload, immediately purchased. great lil' game.

Nice game, does a lot of things really well. 
Overall I think Wallworld is the more interesting approach to this kind of gameplay loop because things are more dynamic and fast-paced, but this game does enough things differently that it manages to stand on its own.
Different characters offer different ways of playing and there is a variety of unlocks.
Good in solo, great in multiplayer. Polished and you can feel developers care about it.
Cool and chill and beastmaster exists and beast master is tuffer than the world and the world yes.

Broken as hell. Game does not save progress at all. Every time I back away to the title screen I have to go through the new game option erasing things I have unlocked. Sucks cause the game is amazing otherwise, but I cant make any progress at all.

best steam sale purchase of 2026.
Not as adicting as...well BALATRO, but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ this game is fun.
haven't died once, its been 3 runs without dying.
I recomend normal mode for beginers.

Used to work, now it doesn't even launch. Spent an hour going through half a dozen guides to fix it, nothing worked. I shouldn't have to spend an hour trying to get a game like this to be functioning.
Freezes on startup.  Followed all the tips online and uninstalled/reinstalled it.  Nothing helped.  I cant give a good rating to a game that doesn't work.

I like the game, but I do not think the price tag is worth it. 

There's only two characters in the base game; you need to buy  DLCs to get two more characters.

The art style is pretty, the music is great, and the general gameplay loop is a cool concept. But I think how the base game is right now, it is not worth buying full price. I'd like to play with my friends, but I couldn't ask them to spend $18 on a game with two characters, and one map (the maps look different, but are functionally the same). 

I feel like the game should be listed as a beta instead of as a full game because it seems incomplete to me.

I can't recommend this game for one reason only. If you have multiple monitors the mouse cannot be locked within the game by default and requires 3rd party programs to solve. The developer is aware of this issue and on forums has indicated that they believe the problem is too minor to "waste" time fixing it. Other have suggested programming solutions that appear extremely simple. I cannot speak on how easy, but the developers refuse to fix it and this has caused issues while playing the game which has caused graphics crashes, closed browser windows, deleted work, etc. Having your mouse wander off screen and not realize it until it has closed or broken something is a huge issue. There is no excuse other than lazyness for this issue to not be fixed by now when they have been aware for years.

If that game taught me something, that is: 
If you have problems, the answer is SLAVERY

The trappings of an indie classic are all here

- lovely colourful pixelly graphics
- some evocative art, design and sound
- wonderfully polished ui

but it's all in service of a game that is extremely tedious to play. 

You dig down more or less at random

you find gubbins

you bring gubbins back up

Every now and then you have to defend the dome, which is extremely simplistic 

Then you go back for digging for gubbins

unlock bigger holes to dig in

unlock different dome types

unlock different characters

but you'll dig for gubbins

Then you'll defend the dome

and that's at. the higher difficulties just punish you more for not upgrading the dome weapons

Simple games can be rewarding, but there's just not enough depth to this dig-a-thon.

Bought on 70% discount so I'm not gonna refund, likely even play it a little more, but long story short, it gets repetitive far too fast. There's [i]some[/i] variety to it, but starting over the grind from scratch every single run and playing the same thing over and over again without progress is what kills this game - with the annoyingly slow movement and mining speed specifically.

I've played [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/3631290/Slots__Daggers/][b]Slots & Daggers[/b][/url] recently (reviewed [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/unqlfife/recommended/3631290][b]here[/b][/url] if interested) and I found that game awesome for this very difference: "Roguelike", repetitive, but there's an upgrade group that survives runs, therefore, every new run goes a bit easier on lower tiers and one can reach higher ones, a little plus here and there so one gets a little further every run.

This game seem to be missing that, hence it's losing its charm. Maybe the other dome types or characters or something give a little plus but... So far it feels like it won't.



Feel free to follow my Curator Page,
[url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/46155089-Hooked-Or-Horrified/][b]Hooked or Horrified[/b][/url]
for future updates and new game reviews.
The difficulty is either extremely easy or extremely challenging, the middle ground is rare and it makes it hard to go back for another run

A wonderfull game. If you like rougelikes and tower defense, it´s your ticket! I very like it and it´s good and nice to play with your friends, if you want a cozy and chill game to play, don´t matter with friends or not, it´s worth the money.

The game is pretty decent with resource managment of balancing on getting upgrades for the dome so you dont die to enemies and getting enough upgrades for the keeper so you can dig and get resources faster as the terrain progressively gets harder to dig through. the terrain also has different densities making some rock easier to break so the most optimal way to mine isnt just making a minecraft strip mine, but following weakspots on the rock to dig faster. 

But my biggest complaint is about the characters, well specifically the starting one. why does a character named the ENGINEER not have the ability to construct anything to help with mining or carrying ♥♥♥♥????? like the other "space guy" i forgot the name of comes with ping pong orbs that make digging a more interesting mechanic than just slamming your big fat drill into the rock for the 5000th time. ontop of having INFINITE CARRY CAPACITY FROM THE SECOND YOU LOAD IN???!!!?!?!?!

I havent played the DLC but I would assume they also have similar unique mechanics similar to "space guy" with some kind of upgrade trees and ♥♥♥♥, It just really boggles my mind that why the engineer guy doesnt get ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, like imagine being able to have some kind of decision between transport machines, digging machines and maybe some kind of overclock buff. transport stuff could be like a conveyor you can set up with some thinking involved and then the digging machine could just be a drill that digs for a time, maybe needing upkeep if you wanna keep it diggin
